name: Update Product Details Load Test (Full Replace)
description: Test the /api/v1/products/{product_id} endpoint under 30 concurrent virtual users performing PUT operations for 5 minutes to validate response time, data consistency, and error rate requirements for the full product update (replace) operation.
corresponding NFR: The /api/v1/products/{product_id} endpoint must maintain a 90th percentile response time of ≤ 700 ms, an error rate of < 1%, and data consistency (updates must reflect in subsequent GET requests) under 30 concurrent PUT operations.
preconditions: The application is deployed and fully accessible in a stable test environment.
A test database snapshot with a known pool of pre-existing product IDs is prepared and restored before the test run.
All virtual users hold a valid Bearer authentication token included in the Authorization header.
Tokens must be pre-generated before the test and have a TTL exceeding the total test duration.
Each virtual user targets a distinct product_id from the pre-seeded pool to avoid update contention.

metrics.unit: % of updates reflected in subsequent GETs

notes: A 30 s ramp-up duration is a proposed default to prevent write contention spikes at test start. Steady-state duration of 300 s is a proposed default. Data consistency validation requires a follow-up GET request after each PUT to confirm the update was persisted — this must be implemented as an assertion in the test script. Bearer tokens must be pre-generated with a TTL greater than the total test duration (ramp-up + steady-state = 330 s minimum).
